See examples below A1: Correct shape that cuts x-axis once either side of the origin, maximum in quadrant 2 and y intercept (0, 11) or 11 marked on y-axis. Condone (11, 0) if the intercept is in the correct place. Do not be concerned with any labelled x-intercepts. Condone aspects of the graph which may appear linear (but not a ^ shape). Its line of symmetry must appear to the left of the origin and its curve should broadly appear symmetrical about this line. Ignore x-intercepts or the maximum stated. The y-intercept may be stated underneath their graph instead, but if there is a contradiction between the graph and what is marked on the graph then the graph takes precedence. Do not accept graphs which appear to be symmetrical about the y-axis. Examples M1A1 M1A0 M0A0 (c) B1ft: Correct equation seen in part (c) (follow through their numeric c so allow x = έΙΤ έΑεcέΑζ).
